name: Cloudflare Open Redirect Parameter Guard Pack
objective: Detect and block open-redirect abuse by inspecting common redirect parameters for external absolute URLs and protocol-relative targets.
risk_mitigated: Prevents phishing pivot and trust-abuse flows where attackers weaponize first-party redirect endpoints to send users to malicious destinations.

rules.description: Match common redirect parameters when value includes an external absolute URL or protocol-relative target.
rules.expression: (http.request.uri.query contains "next=http://" or http.request.uri.query contains "next=https://" or http.request.uri.query contains "next=//" or http.request.uri.query contains "url=http://" or http.request.uri.query contains "url=https://" or http.request.uri.query contains "url=//" or http.request.uri.query contains "redirect_uri=http://" or http.request.uri.query contains "redirect_uri=https://" or http.request.uri.query contains "redirect_uri=//" or http.request.uri.query contains "returnUrl=http://" or http.request.uri.query contains "returnUrl=https://" or http.request.uri.query contains "returnUrl=//")

deployment_notes: Deploy in log/challenge-compatible observation mode first (for example, clone rule with action set to log where available) for at least 7 days.
Review hit samples for legitimate identity-provider or partner redirects before enforcing block.
After tuning exceptions, switch enforcement action to block for high-confidence abuse prevention.
Roll out in phases (staging -> low-risk production paths -> full production).
tuning_guidance: Allowlist approved redirect destinations for known SSO/OAuth paths (for example, /oauth/callback, /sso/complete) where absolute redirect_uri usage is expected.
If your application encodes redirect targets, add explicit exceptions for safe signed/validated redirect tokens rather than broad URI exclusions.
Prefer app-side validation (same-origin or strict allowlist) and keep WAF logic as a compensating control.
Exclude trusted administrative or internal workflows only when business owners confirm necessity and logging remains enabled.
monitoring_notes: Track rule hit rate by URI path and parameter name (next, url, redirect_uri, returnUrl) to identify abused endpoints.
Alert on sudden spikes from single ASNs/IPs or campaigns targeting login and logout flows.
Correlate blocks with downstream phishing reports, account takeover attempts, and anomalous referral telemetry.
Periodically review false positives after identity platform or routing changes.
